---
title: "Training Overview"
---

Training runs on GPU-accelerated Kubernetes clusters via Azure ML and NVIDIA OSMO. The toolchain
supports both reinforcement learning (Isaac Lab + SKRL) and imitation learning (LeRobot).

![Robotic systems learn manipulation tasks through iterative training in simulated environments.
*Image: [Humanoid robot](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:ASIMO_4.28.11.jpg)
by Vanillase, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/)*
](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/05/ASIMO_4.28.11.jpg/429px-ASIMO_4.28.11.jpg){fig-alt="Honda ASIMO humanoid robot" width="60%"}

## Training Frameworks

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-training-pipelines
%%| fig-cap: "Reinforcement Learning and Imitation Learning Training Pipelines"
flowchart LR
    subgraph RL["Reinforcement Learning"]
        ISIM[Isaac Sim] --> ILAB[Isaac Lab]
        ILAB --> SKRL[SKRL Agent]
        SKRL --> CKPT_RL[Checkpoint]
    end

    subgraph IL["Imitation Learning"]
        DATA[Demo Data] --> LRBT[LeRobot]
        LRBT --> ACT[ACT Policy]
        LRBT --> DIFF[Diffusion Policy]
        ACT --> CKPT_IL[Checkpoint]
        DIFF --> CKPT_IL
    end

    CKPT_RL --> MLF[MLflow Registry]
    CKPT_IL --> MLF
    MLF --> DEPLOY[Edge Deployment]
```

| Framework | Type | Use Case |
|-----------|------|----------|
| **SKRL** | Reinforcement Learning | Primary RL framework for Isaac Lab tasks |
| **RSL-RL** | Reinforcement Learning | Alternative RL framework |
| **LeRobot ACT** | Imitation Learning | Action Chunking Transformer policy |
| **LeRobot Diffusion** | Imitation Learning | Diffusion-based policy |

## Submitting a Training Job

### Via Azure ML

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Submit RL training to Azure ML
az ml job create \
  --file workflows/azureml/rl-training-job.yml \
  --resource-group rg-nvidia-dev-001 \
  --workspace-name mlw-nvidia-dev-001 \
  --set display_name="cartpole-ppo-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)" \
  --set environment_variables.ACCEPT_EULA="Y" \
  --set environment_variables.PRIVACY_CONSENT="Y"
```

### Via OSMO

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
# Login to OSMO
osmo login https://osmo.example.com --method dev --username guest

# Submit workflow
osmo workflow submit \
  --name rl-cartpole \
  --config workflows/osmo/rl-training.yml
```

## Experiment Tracking with MLflow

Training metrics are automatically logged to Azure ML via MLflow:

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: false
import mlflow
from skrl.agents.torch.ppo import PPO

# MLflow monkey-patches agent._update for metric interception
# Logging intervals: "step", "balanced" (default), "rollout", or integer

mlflow.log_params({
    "algorithm": "PPO",
    "num_envs": 4096,
    "learning_rate": 3e-4,
    "clip_range": 0.2,
    "entropy_coef": 0.01,
})
```

## GPU Requirements

| GPU | Driver | MIG | Notes |
|-----|--------|-----|-------|
| H100 | GPU Operator datacenter | Disabled | Standard |
| RTX PRO 6000 | Azure GRID DaemonSet | `single` (required) | `nvidia.com/gpu.deploy.driver=false` |

::: {.callout-warning}
`NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all` is **required** for all GPU workloads — Isaac Sim
needs Vulkan for rendering.
:::

{{< include ../../../../docs/training/README.md >}}
